data.narrative:
Newcolo is a Series B nuclear energy company that has raised €537M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 comparable companies across the nuclear energy sector: 3 public entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms, including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo among the most visible.

Traditional peer-median valuation proves inapplicable here due to insufficient clustering within narrow cohorts. The nuclear energy sector contains only one same-stage Series B comparable and one adjacent-stage peer, falling short of the minimum three datapoints required for statistically meaningful median derivation. Expanding to all eight private nuclear peers would introduce stage and scale heterogeneity that would obscure rather than illuminate valuation guidance. Similarly, the small public comp set of three firms exhibits material divergence in business model, maturity, and regulatory pathway, limiting their direct applicability.

The fragmented comp landscape reflects a sector-wide characteristic: nuclear technology companies remain highly differentiated by reactor design, fuel type, and commercialization timeline, limiting true peer equivalence. A credible valuation range for Newcolo would require methods beyond peer multiples—including DCF analysis informed by project-level deployment timelines, technology risk weighting, and regulatory capital requirements. The €537M already deployed signals significant investor conviction, but forward valuation depends critically on near-term technology validation milestones and pathway clarity to commercial operation.
